title: Feed updates have no native WebSocket subscription and require polling or a notification side channel
content:
## Claim

The Bee HTTP API exposes WebSocket subscriptions only for PSS, GSOC, and chunk streaming — **feed updates have no native WS subscription**, so real-time feed watching must be built out of other primitives.

## Evidence

WebSocket endpoints:

| Endpoint                        | Purpose                             |
|---------------------------------|-------------------------------------|
| `WS /pss/subscribe/{topic}`     | Stream decrypted PSS messages       |
| `WS /gsoc/subscribe/{address}`  | Stream GSOC updates                 |
| `WS /chunks/stream`             | Stream chunk uploads (parallel)     |

Options for watching feed updates:
1. **Poll** `GET /feeds/{owner}/{topic}` at intervals (what the adapter does today)
2. **GSOC notification**: publisher sends a GSOC on update; subscriber polls only on notification
3. **PSS notification**: same pattern via PSS instead of GSOC
4. **Advance retrieve requests** (protocol level, not API-exposed): request the next feed index before it exists; the node holds the request until the update arrives — gives sub-second latency without polling

## Why it matters

Explains why sub-second collaboration latency needs a sidecar notification channel (GSOC or PSS) rather than just a WebSocket on the feed. Also flags "advance retrieve requests" as a future path if exposed in bee-js.
description: Bee WS only streams PSS, GSOC, and chunk uploads. Real-time feed watching requires polling, or combining with PSS/GSOC notifications, or advance retrieve requests.
